content_markdown:
A uniform rod PQ of weight $W$ is attached to a vertical wall at end P by a hinge. A force of magnitude $F$ acts on the rod at end Q as shown such that the rod is in equilibrium.

Which of the following diagrams best shows the correct direction of the reaction force $R$ at the hinge?

diagram_metadata.description: A diagram showing a rod PQ hinged at P to a wall, with a horizontal force F acting left at Q and weight W acting downwards from the center.

vibe_explanation: For a rigid body in equilibrium, the sum of forces must be zero. Since force $F$ acts to the left, the reaction force $R$ must have a rightward component to balance it. Since weight $W$ acts downwards, $R$ must have an upward component to balance it. Furthermore, for three non-parallel forces to be in equilibrium, their lines of action must intersect at a single point. Extending the lines of $F$ and $W$ shows they intersect above and to the right of P, meaning $R$ must point towards that intersection point (up and to the right).
